title: Quantum Computing Progress 2026
body:
# Introduction

Quantum computing has made significant strides in 2026, with breakthroughs in error correction and qubit stability. This article reviews the key developments reported in major physics journals and industry announcements. The progress spans hardware improvements, algorithmic advances, and commercial partnerships.

# Bibliography

- Quantum Computing Advances, 2026
- Breaking Quantum Limits, Physical Review Letters, 2025
- Smith & Brown, Nature Physics, 2026
- Quantum Error Correction Milestones, 2025
- IBM Quantum Roadmap Update, 2026
- Google Sycamore 2026 benchmark

title: APA 7th Edition Citation Style Guide
body:
# APA 7th Edition Citation Style

The APA 7th edition citation style uses an author-date format. In-text citations include the author's last name and the year of publication. The reference list entry at the end should contain the following elements: Author, A. A., & Author, B. B. (Year). Title of the article. *Title of the Periodical, volume*(issue), pages. DOI

## Reference Format Rules
- Authors: Last name, first initial. Separate multiple authors with commas, use an ampersand (&) before the last author.
- Year: In parentheses, directly after the authors.
- Title: Sentence case, not in quotes.
- Journal: Italicized, title case, followed by a comma.
- Volume: Italicized, directly after the journal.
- Issue: In parentheses, not italicized, immediately after volume.
- Pages: Preceded by "pp." for newspaper articles, but for journal articles just page range.
- DOI: If available, provide as a hyperlink: https://doi.org/xxxx

Example:
Smith, J., & Brown, A. (2026). Quantum computing breakthroughs. *Nature Physics*, 22(3), 45-52. https://doi.org/10.1038/nphys.2026.1
